retry_reason: Lookup failure (-ENOENT) indicates the conntrack entry is not present; it may appear later if the network connection is established, so the caller may retry after a delay.
contract_summary: This is a get-wrapper that looks up a connection tracking entry (nf_conn) by tuple parameters and returns it with an acquired reference (via nf_conntrack_find_get, which increments the refcount). The caller must eventually call nf_ct_put() to release the reference. The function also handles optional net namespace resolution and sets the direction in opts. On error, it returns an ERR_PTR; the caller must check the return value. Any temporarily held net namespace reference is released internally. The wrapper does not transfer ownership of the net reference.

check.reason: The function returns ct (struct nf_conn *) obtained via nf_ct_tuplehash_to_ctrack, which holds a reference from nf_conntrack_find_get. The access path from return variable ct through ct_general.use.refs matches the callee's type chain. Segment counts (path 4, type chain 4) are consistent.
